text: There is something genuinely satisfying about the feel of a fresh bar of soap — that smooth, slightly waxy resistance under your fingertips before it has been used even once. It has a kind of dense solidity to it, almost like a river stone, but warmer and with that faint give when you press your thumbnail into it. The edges are crisp and perfectly geometric in a way that feels almost too deliberate for something so ordinary. Once it gets wet for the first time and loses that pristine quality, you can never quite get it back, which makes that first touch feel oddly precious.
